title: The Agricultural Produce (Grading and Marking) Act, 1937
title_en: The Agricultural Produce (Grading and Marking) Act, 1937
description: An Act to provide for the grading and marking of agricultural and other produce. WHEREAS it is expedient to provide for the grading and marking of Agricultural and other produce; It is hereby enacted as follows:-

provisions.title: 1. Short title and extent
provisions.content:
1. (1) This Act may be called the Agricultural Produce (Grading and Marking) Act, 1937 .
(2) It extends to the whole of [Bangladesh].

provisions.title: 2. Explanations
3. Prescription of grade designations
4. Penalty for unauthorised marking with grade designation mark
provisions.content: 4. Whoever marks any scheduled article with a grade designation mark, not being authorised to do so by rule made under section 3, shall be punishable with fine which may extend to five hundred [Taka].

provisions.title: 5. Penalty for counterfeiting grade designation mark
provisions.content: 5. Whoever counterfeits any grade designation mark or has in his possession any die, plate or other instrument for the purpose of counterfeiting a grade designation mark shall be punishable with imprisonment which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.

provisions.title: 6. Extension of application of Act
provisions.content: 6. The [Government], after such consultation as it thinks fit of the interests likely to be affected, may by notification in the official Gazette declare that the provisions of this Act shall apply to an article of agricultural produce not included in the Schedule or to an article other than an article of agricultural produce, and on the publication of such notification such article shall be deemed to be included in the Schedule.

definitions.definition: a designation prescribed as indicative of the quality of any scheduled articles
a mark prescribed as representing a particular grade designation
prescribed by rules made under this Act
an article included in the Schedule
